# Termy Documentation

> Termy is a fast, GPU-accelerated terminal emulator built with Rust.

## Quick Links

- Website: https://termy.run
- Repository: https://github.com/lassejlv/termy
- Documentation: https://termy.run/docs

## Overview

Termy is a modern terminal emulator focused on speed, minimal resource usage, and a clean native experience.
It starts in ~8ms, uses ~18MB of memory, and runs natively on macOS, Windows, and Linux.

## Documentation Sections

### Guides

- [Building from Source](https://termy.run/docs/building-from-source) — Build Termy from source code for development or unsupported platforms
- [Customization Guide](https://termy.run/docs/customization-guide) — Deep customization of fonts, themes, appearance, and behavior

### Getting Started

- [Configuration](https://termy.run/docs/configuration) — Configure Termy settings and appearance
- [First Steps](https://termy.run/docs/first-steps) — Your complete onboarding guide to becoming productive with Termy
- [Installation](https://termy.run/docs/installation) — Complete guide to installing Termy on macOS, Windows, or Linux
- [Keybindings](https://termy.run/docs/keybindings) — Keyboard shortcuts and customization

### Help & Troubleshooting

- [FAQ](https://termy.run/docs/faq) — Frequently asked questions about Termy
- [Troubleshooting](https://termy.run/docs/troubleshooting) — Solutions to common problems and issues

## Key Features

- ⚡ **Fast**: GPU-accelerated rendering, ~8ms startup
- 🪶 **Lightweight**: ~18MB memory footprint
- 🎨 **Themable**: Built-in themes + custom colors
- ⌨️ **Configurable**: Simple text-based config
- 🖥️ **Native**: Runs natively on macOS, Windows, Linux

## Quick Start

```bash
# macOS
brew install --cask termy-org/termy/termy

# Arch Linux
paru -S termy-bin
```

---

For complete documentation, see https://termy.run/docs
For full LLM context, use llms-full.txt
